# htpasswd-generator
Description: Generate or verify Apache-compatible htpasswd bcrypt hashes (e.g. for nginx/Apache basic auth).
Source: project
Triggers: htpasswd, basic auth, apache password, nginx basic auth, bcrypt hash, htaccess password
Tools: run_skill_script
Scripts: hash.py
# htpasswd-generator

Generate or verify Apache-compatible bcrypt htpasswd hashes. Use this when setting up HTTP Basic Auth on nginx, Apache, Traefik, Caddy, or similar reverse proxies.

## When to use

- User asks for an htpasswd line, htaccess password, basic auth credentials
- Setting up a protected route on nginx / Apache / Traefik
- Verifying that a known password matches an existing bcrypt hash
- Generating a bcrypt hash for any reason (it's the same algorithm)

## How to use

Call `run_skill_script` with `skill_name="htpasswd-generator"` and `script="hash.py"`.

### Modes

#### Generate a full htpasswd line (default)

```
args=["--user","admin","--password","s3cret"]
```

Output:
```
admin:$2b$10$...
```

#### Generate a hash with auto-generated password

```
args=["--user","admin","--auto","--length","24"]
```

Outputs the password (so you can copy it) AND the htpasswd line.

#### Just the bcrypt hash, no username prefix

```
args=["--password","s3cret","--format","hash"]
```

#### Verify a password matches a hash

```
args=["--password","s3cret","--verify","$2b$10$abcdef..."]
```

Exit code 0 = match, 1 = no match.

### Args

| Flag | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--user NAME` | (required for line format) | Username for the htpasswd line |
| `--password PW` | — | Password to hash. Mutually exclusive with `--auto` |
| `--auto` | off | Auto-generate a strong password (prints it on its own line first) |
| `--length N` | 24 | Length when `--auto` is set |
| `--cost N` | 10 | Bcrypt cost factor (Apache default is 10; 12 is more secure but slower) |
| `--format FMT` | `htpasswd` | `htpasswd` (user:hash) or `hash` (just the bcrypt) |
| `--verify HASH` | — | Verify mode — check if `--password` matches `HASH` |
| `--prefix b` | off | Use `$2b$` prefix (modern, recommended). Default is `$2b$` from bcrypt lib |

### Apache compatibility note

Apache `htpasswd -B` produces `$2y$` prefixed hashes, but `$2b$` works identically in all modern Apache/nginx versions. The `bcrypt` library produces `$2b$`. If you need `$2y$` exactly, pass `--prefix y` (the script will swap the prefix; the hash is bit-compatible).
